description_long: This is a double-headed battle axe with a broad, symmetrical blade on each side, both featuring intricate gold Celtic knotwork patterns. The blades are metallic with a darkened steel finish and a sharp, polished edge, while the central section where the blades meet the handle is adorned with decorative gold bands and a blue gemstone. The handle is straight, cylindrical, and appears to be made of dark wood or a wood-like material, reinforced with gold metal bands and capped at the base with a gold ring. The axe has a robust and ornamental appearance, suggesting it could be ceremonial or for display, but its construction also implies functional use. The overall design is medieval-inspired, with a focus on both aesthetics and durability. The object is visually striking, with a harmonious blend of dark and metallic tones accented by gold and blue highlights.
description: A double-headed battle axe with ornate gold Celtic patterns, a dark wooden handle, and decorative gold accents. The design is both functional and highly decorative, with a medieval aesthetic.
description_view_0: Front view showing both symmetrical blades with gold Celtic knotwork and the central blue gemstone.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the blades and the pointed tip at the top of the handle.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, displaying the same blade patterns and handle details.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thin blade profile and the pointed handle tip.
attribute_handle_type: Straight cylindrical handle, likely wood, reinforced with gold bands, smooth surface, round cross-section, capped with a gold ring.
attribute_blade_design: Double-headed symmetrical blades with wide, curved edges, gold Celtic knotwork, sharp edges, no beard, and a central decorative section with a blue gemstone.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with visible gold bands securing the blade to the handle.
